name: Cytokine and Inflammasome Modulation Registry Metadata Refresh

scope.question: Do current ClinicalTrials.gov, ANZCTR, and PubMed checks for stale Cytokine and Inflammasome Modulation trial-watch records change the public outlook or only clear local registry metadata debt?

summary: Ran a bounded Cytokine and Inflammasome Modulation registry metadata refresh after the scoped trial-watch audit showed seven registry-linked studies, six stale or missing registry checks, and zero open stale watch items. Direct ClinicalTrials.gov API checks refreshed CANTOS canakinumab NCT01327846, COLCOT colchicine NCT02551094, RESCUE ziltivekimab NCT03926117, POSIBIL6ESKD/clazakizumab NCT05485961, dapansutrile HFrEF NCT03534297, and the ZEUS ziltivekimab watch item NCT05021835; ANZCTR review refreshed LoDoCo2 ACTRN12614000093684. No known registry posted a new result that changes the public outlook. Broad registry search surfaced the completed no-results RIGHT clazakizumab older-adult physical-function trial NCT05727384 and pre-window posted-result ziltivekimab Japan registry NCT04626505 as future coverage-repair context rather than current metadata-refresh public changes.

materiality_decision.rationale: This pass cleared stale local trial-details metadata for existing scoped records. Current registry and PubMed checks did not add new cardiovascular outcome, frailty, older-adult function, direct NLRP3 outcome, mortality, broad aging, correction, or retraction evidence beyond the already represented public baseline. The close RIGHT older-adult clazakizumab registry has no posted results, while the ziltivekimab Japan and CHIP/inflammasome registries are source-completeness or taxonomy-boundary context for a later coverage-repair pass.

search_log.summary: Scoped report contained 7 registry-linked studies, 0 missing trial-detail records, 6 stale or missing registry checks, 0 open stale watch items, and 1 active watch item.

search_log.query: Direct checks for NCT01327846, NCT05485961, NCT02551094, NCT03534297, NCT03926117, and NCT05021835
search_log.summary: CANTOS, COLCOT, and RESCUE remained completed with posted ClinicalTrials.gov results. Dapansutrile HFrEF remained completed with no ClinicalTrials.gov results section and is still represented by its peer-reviewed source. NCT05485961 remained recruiting as the combined POSIBIL6ESKD phase 2b/3 clazakizumab outcomes study with no posted registry results. ZEUS remained active-not-recruiting with no posted results and estimated June 9, 2026 primary and study completion.

LoDoCo2 remained completed in ANZCTR, with date last updated March 22, 2021, actual last data collection February 17, 2020, and final sample size 5522. The peer-reviewed NEJM result remains the public evidence source.

search_log.query: ziltivekimab OR clazakizumab OR dapansutrile OR OLT1177 OR canakinumab OR colchicine, Entrez dates 2026/06/08 to 2026/06/14
search_log.summary: Exact intervention-name PubMed search found no known-registry publication and no public-stage-changing result. Close hits included anti-IL-1 guideline updates, an OLT1177/dapansutrile macrophage preprint, a preclinical dapansutrile sepsis cardiac-injury paper, a clazakizumab transplant comment, colchicine case or adherence context, and wrong-scope inflammatory disease records.

search_log.query: NCT01327846 OR NCT02551094 OR NCT03926117 OR NCT05485961 OR NCT03534297 OR NCT05021835 OR ACTRN12614000093684, Entrez dates 2026/06/08 to 2026/06/14; baseline PMIDs with retraction, erratum, corrected-and-republished, or expression-of-concern publication types
search_log.summary: Known-registry same-window search returned zero PubMed records, and the correction/retraction screen returned zero records for the baseline supporting PMIDs.

search_log.query: ziltivekimab; clazakizumab; dapansutrile OR OLT1177 OR NLRP3 inflammasome inhibitor; canakinumab colchicine cardiovascular inflammation aging frailty older adults; IL-6 inhibition cardiovascular chronic kidney inflammation phase 3; inflammasome inhibitor aging frailty older adults
search_log.summary: Broad registry searches returned represented studies plus no-results or boundary context. The closest same-window registry activity was RIGHT NCT05727384, a completed clazakizumab older-adult physical-function trial updated June 11, 2026 with no posted results. Other close context included ziltivekimab Japan NCT04626505 with posted results first posted May 6, 2026 and DFV890/MAS825 CHIP inflammation registry NCT06097663 with posted results first posted March 27, 2026; these predate the current delta window and belong in a future coverage-repair or taxonomy-boundary pass if prioritized.

trial_watch_checks.summary: CANTOS remained completed with posted ClinicalTrials.gov results and no current registry movement. Local metadata now records registry freshness, actual completion dates, and results first-posted date.

The peer-reviewed phase 2b biomarker paper remains the public evidence source, while ClinicalTrials.gov now carries the recruiting POSIBIL6ESKD phase 2b/3 cardiovascular outcomes study with no posted registry results and a last update posted June 8, 2026. This remains watch-context, not a public evidence change.

COLCOT remained completed with posted ClinicalTrials.gov results and no new registry outcome delta. Local metadata now records registry freshness, actual completion dates, and results first-posted date.

The dapansutrile HFrEF registry remained completed with no ClinicalTrials.gov results section and a last update posted January 9, 2020. The peer-reviewed PMID 33235030 paper remains the public evidence source.

LoDoCo2 remained completed in ANZCTR, with date last updated March 22, 2021, actual last data collection February 17, 2020, and final sample size 5522. The peer-reviewed NEJM result remains the public evidence source.

RESCUE remained completed with posted ClinicalTrials.gov results, actual completion on June 26, 2020, results first posted August 9, 2023, and a last update posted December 31, 2025. No public interpretation changed.

ZEUS remained active-not-recruiting with no ClinicalTrials.gov results section, a last update posted April 13, 2026, and estimated primary and study completion on June 9, 2026. It remains the main near-term IL-6 outcomes watch item.

excluded_sources.title: Improving Physical Function in Older Adults Using an Anti-inflammation Drug: The RIGHT Study
excluded_sources.reason: Completed clazakizumab phase 2 older-adult physical-function registry, last update posted June 11, 2026, with no posted results. It is highly relevant trial-horizon context, but staging a new public source/study should be handled in a separate coverage-repair or trial-layer pass.

excluded_sources.title: Trial to Evaluate Reduction in Inflammation in Patients With Advanced Chronic Renal Disease Utilizing Antibody Mediated IL-6 Inhibition in Japan.
excluded_sources.reason: Completed ziltivekimab Japan phase 2 registry with posted results first posted May 6, 2026. It predates this delta window and should be considered in a future source-completeness pass rather than this stale metadata refresh.

excluded_sources.title: DFV890 and MAS825 for inflammatory marker reduction in coronary heart disease and CHIP
excluded_sources.reason: Completed inflammasome/IL-1 pathway registry in coronary heart disease with CHIP and posted results first posted March 27, 2026. It is taxonomy-boundary and source-completeness context rather than a same-window cytokine metadata-refresh public change.

excluded_sources.title: OLT1177 (Dapansutrile) inhibits Gasdermin D-dependent IL-1beta Release and Pyroptotic Cell Death in Bone Marrow-derived Macrophages.
excluded_sources.reason: Preprint/cell-mechanism dapansutrile context, not a human clinical or aging-functional result.

excluded_sources.title: Dapansutrile alleviates sepsis-induced cardiac injury by modulating NF-kappaB-induced inflammatory response and TXNIP/NLRP3/GSDMD-mediated pyroptosis.
excluded_sources.reason: Preclinical sepsis-induced cardiac-injury paper; it does not change the human HFrEF or broad aging interpretation.

excluded_sources.title: Club Rhumatismes et Inflammation guidelines for anti-interleukin-1 therapy: 2026 update.
excluded_sources.reason: Guideline context for anti-IL-1 therapy, not a new result-bearing canakinumab, cardiovascular, frailty, or aging intervention source.

excluded_sources.title: Cytokines as therapeutic targets: Insights from clinical interventions.
excluded_sources.reason: Editorial-level cytokine-therapy context; it does not provide new primary evidence for the scoped public claim.

excluded_sources.title: Comment on clazakizumab in chronic active antibody-mediated kidney transplant rejection.
excluded_sources.reason: Letter/comment on transplant clazakizumab evidence rather than a new result-bearing record for cytokine/inflammasome modulation in aging-relevant cardiovascular, frailty, CKD, or function contexts.

notes: This pass was selected manually from the global stale registry-check report after the State of the Field packet showed no open approval items and the July edition remained date-blocked.
The completed local studies remain posted-result context because their result-bearing peer-reviewed or registry sources are already represented.

NCT05485961 is a boundary case because the local study represents a published phase 2b biomarker paper, while the current ClinicalTrials.gov record also carries the recruiting POSIBIL6ESKD outcomes study. The local metadata was refreshed without treating that recruiting no-results extension as a new public evidence result.

RIGHT/NCT05727384 and ziltivekimab Japan/NCT04626505 are likely worth a future no-results or source-completeness pass, but were not staged here because this pass was scoped to current metadata and same-window deltas.
next_actions: Regenerate the scoped and global trial-watch reports so the State of the Field blocker reflects the refreshed cytokine/inflammasome metadata.

Consider a future Cytokine and Inflammasome Modulation coverage-repair pass for RIGHT/NCT05727384, ziltivekimab Japan/NCT04626505, CHIP inflammasome-boundary records, and the no-results registry layer if source completeness is prioritized.
Continue the stale registry metadata queue with the next coherent track-level batch rather than mixing unrelated tracks in one session.
